lastReleaseMessage: Doc and config follow-up to the v0.14.4 hardening pass. DESIGN.md api sections now describe the shipped behavior: rules validation and real change counts, preview LIKE escaping, transactions_live read paths, clamped analytics params, guardCents on budgets, single-query stats with archived count. Dependabot now ignores react-family major bumps so react and react-dom can only move as a pair, closing the mismatch that PR 7 introduced.

barRaise.topFinding: Full 28-lens fan-out. Runtime code is unchanged since the 2026-07-04 full pass (the only two commits since, 2be93d7 and 40600a3, are a scheduled-task routine rename and a Dependabot tailwindcss-major ignore), so nothing here is a regression: every finding is a real gap that lives at HEAD, and the health dip from 89 to 84 comes from a broader, deeper fan-out surfacing more robustness, test, and doc debt than the carried set, not from anything breaking. No lens returned a BLOCK and all four hard rules hold (D-006 loopback bind at server.ts:1255-1256 with server-bind.test.ts, D-003 guardCents at money boundaries, D-001 no outbound HTTP from importers/, D-004/D-007 BackupFailedError). The verdict stays working: the tool is M6-done at 100 percent, live, and functioning. The strongest signal is a three-lens convergence on bulk-delete (server.ts:534) permanently deleting rows with no pre-delete snapshot, the one destructive path that bypasses the D-004/D-007 snapshot contract every other destructive route honors. A second three-lens convergence confirms push-to-github.ps1:28-32 still blind-deletes .git/index.lock before Assert-GitSafe, and a genuinely new finding is that git-guard.ps1:62 points post-corruption recovery at unstick-git.ps1, a script that does not exist. The four carried MED actions persist (bulk-delete snapshot, CORS wildcard, push blind lock-delete, Discord fifth webhook) and are still undrained because all are auto_safe:false and need an attended release; the cleanest close is one batched Budget release session.

actions.title: Narrow the yesandeverything.com CORS grant (server.ts:90-98) off the wildcard /* to the GET probe route only.
Delete the blind Remove-Item .git\index.lock block in push-to-github.ps1:28-32 and rely on Assert-GitSafe.
Add route/unit tests for goals and budget-target CRUD (createGoal/updateGoal/deleteGoal, setBudgetTarget/clearBudgetTarget).
Tighten package.json engines.node to ^20.19.0 || >=22.12.0 to match the vite 8 / vitest 3 floor.
Fix the git-guard.ps1:62 recovery pointer (unstick-git.ps1 does not exist) to release.ps1 -Bump none.
Harden Plaid sync: add a per-request timeout and make applyAdded fail-soft on a 4-tuple UNIQUE collision so the cursor cannot wedge.
Reconcile the Budget fifth (resources) Discord webhook against the naming standard (file missing, channel routed through the audit webhook).
Bound the subscription detector query to a trailing window so its cost tracks the detection horizon, not lifetime rows.
